growth_records.medium: BG11 (air-bubbled liquid, ambient CO2, no added NaHCO3)
growth_records.cite: Nieves-Morión et al. Retention of a SulP-family bicarbonate transporter in a periplasmic N2-fixing cyanobacterial endosymbiont of an open ocean diatom. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf202. [PMC12596568]
growth_records.snippet: Without added NaHCO3, growth was observed for the WT and only the Δ5 mutant carrying RintRC_3892 (0.55 ± 0.013 day−1 and 0.88 ± 0.006 day−1, respectively) (Table 2).
growth_records.notes: Paper reports growth rate constant mu = ln2/td in day-1 directly (mu=0.55±0.013 day-1 for WT under ambient-air CO2, no added NaHCO3, bubbled BG11, 30C, light). Converted to per-hour: mu_h = 0.55/24 = 0.02292 h-1; doubling_time_h = ln2/0.55*24 = 30.25 h.

growth_records.snippet: Without added NaHCO3, growth was observed for the WT and only the Δ5 mutant carrying RintRC_3892 (0.55 ± 0.013 day−1 and 0.88 ± 0.006 day−1, respectively) (Table 2).

growth_records.cite: Behle et al. Manipulation of topoisomerase expression inhibits cell division but not growth and reveals a distinctive promoter structure in Synechocystis. Nucleic Acids Research (2022). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkac1132. [PMC9825172]
growth_records.snippet: The culture was grown in continuous light at OD750 approx 2.7 and with a dilution rate phi approx 0.24 d-1 ... cnt.: onset of continuous culture (rate phi = 0.01 h-1)
growth_records.notes: Continuous (chemostat) culture at steady state: mu = D. Reported dilution rate phi = 0.24 /d, restated as 0.01 /h. mu = 0.01 /h. doubling = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.01 = 69.3 h (= ln2/0.24 d = 2.89 d). Photoautotroph; light ~90 umol/m2/s per OD750. BG11 composition cited to ref 67, not spelled out -> composition:[]. No numeric specific CO2 uptake or O2 secretion rate given in main text (offgas O2/CO2 monitored but not reported as per-biomass fluxes).

growth_records.cite: Hiroki Nishiguchi et al. Novel allosteric inhibition of phosphoribulokinase identified by ensemble kinetic modeling of Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803 metabolism. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2020.e00153. [PMC7721636]
growth_records.snippet: The specific cell growth rate of the Et-g strains (0.013 ± 0.000 h-1) was 19% lower than that of the wild type strains (0.016 ± 0.000 h-1)... GC-MS analysis of the culture medium of this study showed that no other metabolites were produced
growth_records.notes: Wild-type (GT) reference strain, photoautotrophic batch, carbon source = 50 mM NaHCO3 (inorganic C via CO2). Measured specific growth rate mu = 0.016 +/- 0.000 h-1 (reported). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.016 = 43.3 h. No inorganic-carbon uptake rate or O2 rate quantified numerically in the paper, so uptake_rates left empty. No excreted metabolites detected by GC-MS, so secretion_rates empty. Medium composition given as 'modified BG11' (preculture recipe) used throughout; production/metabolome cultures described as 'BG11 medium containing 50 mM NaHCO3'.

growth_records.strain: Et-g ethanol-producing engineered strain (glgC:Kmr, NS1:PnblA-pdc-adhII-Am; Zymomonas mobilis pdc + adhII overexpressed, glgC/glgX deleted)

sec.method: gas chromatography (GC-FID) of extracellular ethanol; specific production rate
derived from reported 5.2 mg/gDCW/h

growth_records.cite: Hiroki Nishiguchi et al. Novel allosteric inhibition of phosphoribulokinase identified by ensemble kinetic modeling of Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803 metabolism. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2020.e00153. [PMC7721636]
growth_records.snippet: The specific production rate of ethanol was determined to be 5.2 ± 0.0 mg g-DCW-1 h-1 that was similar to the literature value. The specific cell growth rate of the Et-g strains (0.013 ± 0.000 h-1) was 19% lower than that of the wild type s
growth_records.notes: Engineered ethanol-producing strain, photoautotrophic batch on 50 mM NaHCO3. Measured specific ethanol production rate = 5.2 +/- 0.0 mg g-DCW-1 h-1 (reported, GC). Converted to molar: ethanol MW 46.07 g/mol; 5.2 mg/gDCW/h / 46.07 = 0.1129 ~ 0.113 mmol/gDW/h. Measured specific growth rate mu = 0.013 +/- 0.000 h-1 (reported). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.013 = 53.3 h. Ethanol was the only excreted product detected (GC-MS found no other metabolites). No inorganic-carbon (CO2/HCO3-) or O2 uptake rate quantified numerically, so uptake_rates empty. Paper notes the ethanol rate was 'similar to the literature value' but the 5.2 value is the authors' own measurement in this study.

up.method: iSyn811 genome-scale model constraint (net carbon flux) at autotrophic mu=0.09/h and light input 0.8 mE/gDW/h; NOT an experimental measurement

mfa.note: iSyn811 constraint-based simulation constraint, corresponds to light input 0.8 mE/gDW/h; NOT measured
iSyn811 net carbon flux into cell, CO2 as carbon source; NOT measured

product_yields.glycine_betaine_intracellular_max: 64.29 umol/gDW (1.89 umol/mg protein) in delta-ggpS chassis under 3% NaCl at 4 days; intracellular accumulation/titer, NOT a secretion rate

growth_records.cite: Ferreira, Eunice A. et al. Heterologous Production of Glycine Betaine Using Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803-Based Chassis Lacking Native Compatible Solutes. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2021.821075. [PMC8777070]
growth_records.snippet: Simulations were constrained to match an autotrophic specific growth rate of 0.09/h, which corresponds to a light input of 0.8 mE/gDW/h and to a net carbon flux of 3.4 mmol/gDW/h into the cell, with CO2 as carbon source. The maximum glycine
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (cyanobacterium) grown photoautotrophically in BG11 (composition not listed in text; cited Stanier et al. 1971), 30C, pH 8.2 (solid; TES-KOH), 12h/12h light 25 uE/m2/s, 150 rpm, batch. Experimental growth was monitored only as OD730/chlorophyll-a curves; NO numeric measured specific growth rate is reported. The only rate values in the paper (mu=0.09/h; net CO2 flux 3.4 mmol/gDW/h; light 0.8 mE/gDW/h) are iSyn811 genome-scale MODEL constraints, NOT measurements -- flagged is_experimental:false and doubling_time_h=ln2/0.09=7.7 h computed from the model mu only for reference. Glycine betaine (64.29, 12.92, 80.62 umol/gDW etc.) is measured intracellular compatible-solute accumulation (a titer/content), not a secretion or uptake rate, so placed in yields not secretion_rates. Record retained to preserve the defined prokaryote growth condition; no experimental physiological rate available. No fabricated numbers.

growth_records.medium: BG-11 mineral medium supplemented with Na2CO3, CO2-enriched aeration
growth_records.cite: Luimstra, Veerle M et al. Changes in water color shift competition between phytoplankton species with contrasting light-harvesting strategies. Ecology (2020). DOI:10.1002/ecy.2951. [PMC7079016]
growth_records.method: calculated (steady-state chemostat: mu = D = 0.015/h; specific mortality assumed = D)
growth_records.snippet: 'All cultures were grown at 30C in 1.8-L, flat-panel chemostats under light-limited conditions... dilution rate of D = 0.015 h-1.' 'The chemostats were provided with a nutrient-rich mineral medium (BG-11 medium) supplemented with 5 mmol/L N
growth_records.notes: Photoautotrophic light-limited chemostat; Synechocystis PCC 6803 is a prokaryote (cyanobacterium). At steady state specific growth rate equals dilution rate, mu = D = 0.015/h; doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.015 = 46.2 h. No metabolite substrate-uptake or product-secretion rates measured (the limiting resource is light; CO2 supplied in excess as 2% v/v). Only chlorophyll-based competition/photosynthetic-efficiency parameters were fitted. The green alga Chlorella sorokiniana 211-8K in the same study is a eukaryote and is excluded. Blue-light and red-light monocultures share the same fixed D=0.015/h.

growth_records.strain: PCC 6803 (glucose-tolerant), engineered strain pEEK2-ST expressing Kivd S286T
growth_records.medium: BG11 medium + kanamycin + NaHCO3 (sealed flask, medium-light photoautotrophic)

sec.method: GC-FID quantification of secreted product in medium
GC-FID (cumulative, sums product removed in each 2 mL sampling)

sec.units: mg/L cumulative titer at day 46 (max in-flask 105 mg/L at day 40)
sec.method: GC-FID

growth_records.cite: Miao, Rui et al. Enhancement of photosynthetic isobutanol production in engineered cells of Synechocystis PCC 6803. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2018). DOI:10.1186/s13068-018-1268-8. [PMC6158846]
growth_records.method: reported qualitatively via OD750 only (no gDW; no clean specific growth rate extractable from text)
growth_records.snippet: 'A maximal in-flask isobutanol titer of 435 mg l-1 was observed at day 40 when the OD750 was 4.7... the cumulative titer was as high as 911 mg l-1.' 'The highest isobutanol production rate of 43.6 mg l-1 day-1 was observed between days 4 an
growth_records.notes: Engineered photoautotrophic cyanobacterium (prokaryote). Secretion is measured VOLUMETRICALLY (mg/L/day and cumulative mg/L), NOT per biomass (no gDW reported; growth tracked only by OD750). Best condition = HCl-titrated, 50 umol photons/m2/s medium light. Isobutanol and 3M1B are heterologous products of the 2-keto-acid pathway (Kivd S286T from L. lactis). No substrate (CO2/HCO3-) uptake rate quantified per biomass. Cannot compute specific rates (mu, qP) without gDW/timepoint OD values (values only in Fig.2 image). pH set to ~7.5 (target range 7-8). Included for completeness because a measured product secretion rate is reported.

up.compound: light (photons, incident photon flux density I0)
up.method: controlled/measured incident irradiance in photobioreactor; transmitted light I_Z measured

up.compound: CO2 (inorganic carbon, assimilated as bicarbonate HCO3-)
up.method: sole carbon source; air enriched to 5000 ppm CO2; no single measured per-biomass uptake rate reported in this paper (model BOF flux only)

sec.method: measured by Clark-type electrode; O2 evolution determined by stopping gas bubbling and measuring dissolved O2 accumulation; O2 consumption measured shortly after onset of darkness

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: BOF biomass stoichiometry at I0=660 uE/m2/s
mfa.flux: 511.73 mmol photons + 40.85 mmol CO2 + ... -> 1.0 gCDM + 57.04 mmol O2 + byproducts
mfa.note: Model biomass objective function (BOF) stoichiometry estimated at growth rate 0.10 h-1; biomass 23% protein, 20% glycogen; NOT a measured MFA flux

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Maximal biomass yield per photon Y_BM_max
mfa.note: Model/FBA-derived stoichiometric yield; cited experimental comparison Y_BM ~1.0 gCDM/mol photons for Synechocystis PCC 6803 from Touloupakis et al. ref [54]

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: K_L (maximal capacity of light utilization / photon uptake)
mfa.note: Fitted parameter; other fitted params alpha=0.13 m2/gCDM, alpha_b=0.66, k_d=0.07

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Photosynthetic quotient PQ (O2 evolved / CO2 assimilated)

growth_records.cite: Höper et al. A quantitative description of light-limited cyanobacterial growth using flux balance analysis. PLOS Computational Biology (2024). DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1012280. [PMC11326710]
growth_records.snippet: Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803 (sub-strain GT-L) was cultivated in a turbidostat regime (cell density 2-4 x10^7 cells mL-1) using a highly controlled flat-panel photobioreactor FMT-150... grown in BG-11 medium with NO3- as a sole source of nitr
growth_records.notes: FBA/modeling paper (Hoper et al. 2024, PLOS Comput Biol) that reanalyzes a coherent experimental dataset of Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803 GT-L measured previously by Zavrel et al. (cited as ref [1]). EXPERIMENTAL measurements (attributed to that dataset): specific growth rate vs light intensity (from OD680 exponential regression, measured in the photobioreactor), net O2 evolution vs light and dark O2 consumption (Clark-type electrode), areal biomass density, pigment/glycogen/protein content, elemental composition. growth_rate_per_h=0.10 is the measured specific growth rate at the reference incident light I0=660 uE/m2/s (used to estimate the reference BOF). doubling_time computed as ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.10 = 6.93 h. NOTE: the theoretical maximal growth rate mu_max=0.23 h-1 (minimal division time ~3.0 h) is a model-EXTRAPOLATED value from parameter K_L, NOT a measured rate, so it is not used as the record mu. CO2 is the sole carbon source (taken up as HCO3-) but no measured per-biomass uptake rate is given in this paper (only model BOF flux 40.85 mmol CO2/gCDM). BG-11 medium composition is referenced (ref [73]) but not enumerated in-text, so medium_composition_stated=false. pH not stated. Y_BM_max=1.80 gCDM/mol photons is FBA-derived; the cited experimental yield ~1.0 gCDM/mol photons is from Touloupakis et al. ref [54], not this study. All numeric fluxes in mfa_fluxes are model BOF/fitted values, not 13C-MFA fluxes (this is not a 13C-MFA paper).

growth_records.strain: PCC 6803 (wild-type; Pasteur Culture Collection of Cyanobacteria)

up.method: photoautotrophic CO2 fixation inferred from pH rise and 2-3% CO2 supply; no per-biomass rate reported

sec.method: oxygenic photosynthesis (O2 evolved); not quantified as a rate

growth_records.cite: Mohammad Redwanur Rahman et al. Photonic-chemostat engineering for efficient continuous cultivation of cyanobacteria. RSC Advances (2026). DOI:10.1039/d5ra09945e. [PMC12933868]
growth_records.snippet: The fitted mu max for the representative curve (Fig. 4A) was 0.106 day-1, with an X max of 2.27, consistent with photon-limited growth. ... Final dry biomass ranged from 1.1 to 3.0 g L-1, with the highest value obtained at 253 umol photons
growth_records.notes: Batch, low-light regime (99-287 umol photons m-2 s-1); representative best condition 253 umol white. Paper reports mu_max in per-DAY: representative mu_max=0.106 +/- 0.002 day-1 (range across low-light replicates 0.08-0.118 day-1), Xmax=2.27 +/- 0.03 g/L dry biomass (range 1.08-2.27) from Table 1. mu is a Monod fit of measured daily dry-cell-weight (OD-DCW calibration), hence growth_method=calculated. Converted to per-hour for schema: 0.106/day / 24 = 0.004417 h-1. doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.106 = 6.54 day = 156.9 h. Temperature 30 +/- 0.7 C; pH rose 7.0->9.2. No substrate uptake or product secretion rates (mmol/gDW/h) reported anywhere in paper. CO2 fixation only given as aggregate pilot-scale estimate (15-28 g CO2/day for a 120 L system) in Conclusions, not per-condition/per-biomass.

up.method: photoautotrophic; rapid CO2 assimilation inferred from pH rise; no per-biomass rate

sec.method: oxygenic photosynthesis; not quantified

growth_records.cite: Mohammad Redwanur Rahman et al. Photonic-chemostat engineering for efficient continuous cultivation of cyanobacteria. RSC Advances (2026). DOI:10.1039/d5ra09945e. [PMC12933868]
growth_records.snippet: The representative curve (Fig. 4B) showed the highest biomass growth to ~1.85 g L-1, with a fitted mu max of 0.116 day-1 and X max of 1.96, the highest observed across all categories.
growth_records.notes: Batch, moderate-light regime (421-598 umol photons m-2 s-1); representative best 450 umol white (reached ~2.2 g/L by day 15). Table 1: representative mu_max=0.116 +/- 0.003 day-1 (range 0.05-0.116), Xmax=1.96 +/- 0.02 g/L (range 0.87-1.99). Monod fit of measured dry biomass -> growth_method=calculated. Per-hour: 0.116/24 = 0.004833 h-1. doubling_time=ln2/0.116=5.975 day=143.4 h. Temp 30.2 +/- 0.3 C, pH 7.5-10.8. No measured uptake/secretion rates in the paper.

up.method: photoautotrophic; strong CO2 uptake inferred from alkalisation; no per-biomass rate

sec.method: oxygenic photosynthesis; not quantified

growth_records.cite: Mohammad Redwanur Rahman et al. Photonic-chemostat engineering for efficient continuous cultivation of cyanobacteria. RSC Advances (2026). DOI:10.1039/d5ra09945e. [PMC12933868]
growth_records.snippet: The representative curve (Fig. 4C) showed an overall biomass yield of ~0.96 g L-1, but with slightly lower mu max (0.064 day-1) and X max (1.01) than in low or moderate light.
growth_records.notes: Batch, high-light regime (600-1430 umol photons m-2 s-1); representative best 600 umol white (~0.95-1.0 g/L by day 10). Table 1: representative mu_max=0.064 +/- 0.002 day-1 (range across high-light replicates 0.003-0.113), Xmax=1.01 +/- 0.02 g/L (range 0.58-1.01). Higher intensities (953, 1430 umol) gave <0.65 g/L with visible photoinhibition/chlorosis. Monod fit -> growth_method=calculated. Per-hour: 0.064/24=0.002667 h-1. doubling_time=ln2/0.064=10.83 day=259.9 h. Temp 30.3 +/- 1.5 C. No measured uptake/secretion rates.

growth_records.medium: BG11 (continuous feed: BG11 + 2% v/v phosphate buffer)

sec.method: oxygenic photosynthesis; not quantified

growth_records.cite: Mohammad Redwanur Rahman et al. Photonic-chemostat engineering for efficient continuous cultivation of cyanobacteria. RSC Advances (2026). DOI:10.1039/d5ra09945e. [PMC12933868]
growth_records.snippet: The effect of light intensity was evaluated ... at a fixed temperature of 30 C and dilution rate of 0.075 d-1 ... Increasing PPFD to 500-600 umol ... biomass rising to 0.8-0.9 g L-1. A further increase to 600-700 umol ... 0.6-1.1 g L-1.
growth_records.notes: Continuous chemostat, D=0.075 +/- 0.006 day-1, light 600-700 umol white, 30 +/- 0.3 C; steady-state biomass 0.6-1.1 g/L (steady-state = biomass/OD680 varying <5% over three residence times). At steady state specific growth rate mu = D, so mu=0.075 day-1 (growth_method=calculated via mu=D). Per-hour: D=0.075/24=0.003125 h-1; growth_rate_per_h=0.003125. doubling_time=ln2/0.075=9.24 day=221.8 h. Data are steady-state windows extracted from segments of a single long-term continuous culture, not replicate runs. No measured qS/qP.

growth_records.medium: BG11 (continuous feed: BG11 + 2% v/v phosphate buffer)

sec.method: oxygenic photosynthesis; not quantified

growth_records.cite: Mohammad Redwanur Rahman et al. Photonic-chemostat engineering for efficient continuous cultivation of cyanobacteria. RSC Advances (2026). DOI:10.1039/d5ra09945e. [PMC12933868]
growth_records.snippet: Increasing D to 0.125 d-1 produced the highest and most stable biomass, consistently in the range 0.75-1.0 g L-1. ... a narrow operational window of 0.12-0.14 d-1 emerges as the practical compromise between maintaining high biomass concentr
growth_records.notes: Continuous chemostat OPTIMUM, D=0.125 +/- 0.008 day-1, light 600-700 umol white, 30 +/- 0.3 C; steady-state biomass 0.75-1.0 g/L (highest/most stable). mu = D at steady state = 0.125 day-1 (growth_method=calculated via mu=D). Per-hour: 0.125/24=0.005208 h-1. doubling_time=ln2/0.125=5.545 day=133.1 h. Practical operating window 0.12-0.14 day-1. No measured qS/qP.

growth_records.medium: BG11 (continuous feed: BG11 + 2% v/v phosphate buffer)

sec.method: oxygenic photosynthesis; not quantified

growth_records.cite: Mohammad Redwanur Rahman et al. Photonic-chemostat engineering for efficient continuous cultivation of cyanobacteria. RSC Advances (2026). DOI:10.1039/d5ra09945e. [PMC12933868]
growth_records.snippet: Pushing the dilution rate to 0.175 d-1 caused biomass to drop sharply, approaching washout. ... rates above 0.175 d-1 compromised culture stability.
growth_records.notes: Continuous chemostat near washout, D=0.175 +/- 0.009 day-1, light 600-700 umol white, 30 +/- 0.3 C. Biomass dropped sharply (approaching washout), so a true steady state may not be fully established; mu approx = D = 0.175 day-1 only if a residual steady state held (growth_method=calculated via mu=D; note washout caveat). Per-hour: 0.175/24=0.007292 h-1. doubling_time=ln2/0.175=3.96 day=95.06 h. Included as an upper dilution-rate boundary record. No measured qS/qP. Confidence lowered because biomass is unstable near washout.

sec.method: HPLC/analytical of extracellular broth; below detection (<2 uM)
analytical of extracellular broth; below detection (<20 uM)

growth_records.cite: Battaglino, Beatrice et al. Channeling Anabolic Side Products toward the Production of Nonessential Metabolites: Stable Malate Production in Synechocystis sp. PCC6803. ACS Synthetic Biology (2021). DOI:10.1021/acssynbio.1c00440. [PMC8689693]
growth_records.method: reported (measured exponential-phase mu, Table 2) ; doubling time calculated
growth_records.snippet: Delta-me and Delta-me Delta-mdh showed a measurably slower growth rate (0.052 and 0.050 h-1, respectively) in the exponential phase compared to the WT (0.055 h-1). ... neither fumarate nor malate was detected in significant amount in the su
growth_records.notes: Table 2, measured column. Measured exponential-phase mu = 0.055 +/- 0.001 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.055 = 12.60 h. Photoautotrophic: no organic substrate uptake; carbon from CO2 (1% in air, 150 mL/min); energy from light (30->120 umol photons m-2 s-1) - no per-gDW uptake rate reported, so uptake_rates empty. Product yields are per-biomass (Yp/x, mM gDW-1) not per-substrate; WT secretes neither malate nor fumarate (below detection: 2 uM malate, 20 uM fumarate). BG-11 base salt composition not enumerated in text (cited ref 41 van Alphen 2018); only TES buffer and CO2 stated -> medium_composition_stated:false.

growth_records.notes: Table 2 measured column, Delta-me strain (WD169). mu = 0.052 +/- 0.000 h-1 (measured, exponential). td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.052 = 13.33 h. Measured product yields on biomass: malate 0.067 +/- 0.019 mM gDW-1, fumarate 0.274 +/- 0.014 mM gDW-1 (FBA/FVA predicted 0 for both, i.e. experiment exceeded model). Photoautotrophic, CO2 carbon source, no per-gDW substrate uptake reported.

growth_records.snippet: For the Delta-me Delta-mdh strain, the maximum titers of malate and fumarate observed were to 1.2 and 1.0 mM, respectively, after 14 days of batch cultivation ... measured yields malate 0.607 +/- 0.091, fumarate 0.557 +/- 0.002 mM gDW-1 (Ta
growth_records.notes: Table 2 measured column, Delta-me Delta-mdh double knockout (WD170). mu = 0.050 +/- 0.001 h-1 (measured). td = ln2/0.050 = 0.6931/0.050 = 13.86 h. Measured biomass-normalized yields: malate 0.607 +/- 0.091 mM gDW-1 (FVA predicted range 0-0.860), fumarate 0.557 +/- 0.002 mM gDW-1. Maximum extracellular titers after 14 d batch: malate 1.2 mM, fumarate 1.0 mM (max malate:fumarate molar ratio ~1). Dry weight from OD730 via 148 mg L-1 OD-1 conversion factor. Photoautotrophic CO2; no per-gDW substrate uptake reported.

growth_records.method: reported qualitatively (growth curve similar to WT, Fig 3a; no tabulated mu)
growth_records.snippet: The growth curve of Delta-NSI::fumC was similar to that of the WT ... the overexpression of fumC alone in Synechocystis was not sufficient to enhance the secretion of detectable quantities of neither of the two compounds
growth_records.notes: fumC overexpression alone in WT background. Growth described as similar to WT (~0.055 h-1) but not tabulated numerically (Fig 3a). Neither malate nor fumarate secreted above detection limits (2 uM malate, 20 uM fumarate) -> recorded as 0. Included for condition completeness; quantitative growth/production only in Figure 3/S1, not extractable from text.
